---
title: "User Guide"
subtitle: "A comprehensive guide to using extendr"
page-layout: article
toc: true
toc-depth: 0
date: today
date-format: long
---

This user guide focuses on developing R packages that call Rust code and
publishing them to CRAN. A basic understanding of Rust is assumed. If you
are new to Rust, start with the [Rust Basics](../intro-rust/index.qmd)
section.

## Core Concepts

- [The `#[extendr]` macro](extendr-macro.qmd): How to expose Rust functions
  and structs to R.
- [R to Rust type mapping](data-types.qmd): An overview of how R types map
  to extendr types.
- [Conversion to and from R data](conversion.qmd): Converting between R
  and Rust values using `From`, `Into`, and `TryFrom`.

## Type Mapping

- [Scalar Type Mapping](scalars.qmd): Working with R scalar values in Rust.
- [Vector Type Mapping](vectors.qmd): Working with R vectors in Rust.
- [Using HashMaps](collections.qmd): Working with R named lists and
  Rust `HashMap`s.
- [Rust Structs to Lists](into-list.qmd): Exposing Rust structs as R lists.
- [Default function arguments](default-args.qmd): Handling optional and
  default arguments.

## Integrations

- [`serde` integration](serde-integration.qmd): Serializing and deserializing
  Rust types with `serde`.
- [Async with Tokio](tokio.qmd): Using async Rust with Tokio inside extendr.
- [Building for WebR](webr.qmd): Compiling extendr packages for WebR.

## Publishing

- [Publishing to CRAN](cran-publishing.qmd): Steps and considerations for
  submitting an extendr package to CRAN.
- [CRAN's Rust versions](cran-msrv.qmd): Understanding CRAN's minimum
  supported Rust version policy.

## FAQ

- [Frequently Asked Questions](faq.qmd)

## What's *not* in this guide?

This guide is not a comprehensive introduction to Rust. Concepts are
introduced only to explain extendr tools. To learn more about Rust, consult
["The Book"](https://doc.rust-lang.org/stable/book/).

This guide is also not a comprehensive guide to R package development.
Better resources exist for that, notably
[R Packages (2e)](https://r-pkgs.org/). We will point you to relevant
external resources as needed.

## Software requirements

This guide assumes the following versions of necessary software:

- R: {{< var version.r >}}
- Rust {{< var version.rust >}}
- extendr-api {{< var version.extendr >}}
- rextendr {{< var version.rextendr >}}

Please see [Installation Guide](../installation-guide.qmd) if you have not 
already installed this software.
